title: Permutations and Combinations — Fundamentals
objectives: Apply permutation and combination formulas in basic problems
Distinguish when order matters versus when it does not
Use factorial notation and select the correct counting method
theory: Permutation counts arrangements where order matters.
Combination counts selections where order does not matter.
Factorial notation (n!) represents the product of all positive integers up to n.
The same set of items may yield different permutation and combination counts depending on whether order is important.

formulas.explanation: This formula counts ordered arrangements of r objects selected from n.
This formula counts unordered selections of r objects from n.
A combination is the number of permutations divided by the orderings of the chosen objects.
By definition, the factorial of zero equals one, which makes counting formulas consistent.

workedExamples.title: Arrangement of letters
workedExamples.problem: How many different 3-letter words can be formed from the letters A, B, C, D if repetition is not allowed?
steps: We need ordered arrangements of 3 letters from 4, so use permutation.

workedExamples.title: Selecting a committee
workedExamples.problem: From 7 students, how many ways can a committee of 3 be chosen?
steps: Order does not matter when choosing a committee, so use combination.

workedExamples.title: Permutation versus combination
workedExamples.problem: In how many ways can 2 books be arranged on a shelf from 5? How many ways can 2 books be chosen without order?

questions.question: Permutation means:
questions.explanation: Permutation counts different ordered arrangements of items.

questions.question: Combination means:
questions.explanation: Combination counts selections where order is not important.

questions.question: Permutation formula is:
questions.explanation: This is the formula used to count ordered arrangements.

questions.question: Combination formula is:
questions.explanation: This is the formula for unordered selections.

questions.question: In permutation:
questions.explanation: Different orderings count as different permutations.

questions.question: In combination:
questions.explanation: Combinations ignore the order of selected items.

questions.question: AB and BA in permutation:
questions.explanation: In permutations, AB and BA are counted separately.

questions.question: AB and BA in combination:
questions.explanation: Combinations treat AB and BA as the same selection.

questions.question: nC0 equals:
questions.explanation: There is exactly one way to choose nothing from a set.

questions.question: Factorial of 0 is:
How many ordered arrangements are there of 3 letters chosen from A, B, C, D?
How many ways can 4 students be chosen from a class of 8?
If 5 objects are arranged in a line, how many different permutations exist?
From 6 books, how many ways can 2 books be selected for a reading list?
questions.explanation: Use 6C2 = 15 when order does not matter.

questions.question: How many 4-letter words can be formed from the letters A,B,C,D,E without repetition?
From 10 books, in how many ways can 3 books be selected for a shelf if order matters?
How many ways can a committee of 4 be chosen from 12 people?
In how many ways can 5 people be arranged in a row?
How many different 3-digit numbers can be formed from digits 1,2,3,4 if digits cannot repeat?
How many ways can 2 letters be selected from ABCD if order does not matter?
How many ways can 4 different trophies be given to 4 winners?
questions.explanation: This is 4! = 24 ways to assign trophies.

questions.question: From 8 players, how many cricket teams of 4 can be selected?
How many passwords of length 3 can be formed from digits 0-9 if repetition is allowed?
In how many ways can 3 students be seated in 3 chairs chosen from 5 students?
How many diagonals does a convex polygon with 10 vertices have?
How many distinct arrangements are there of the letters in the word LEVEL?
How many ways can 5 prizes be distributed among 7 students if each student can win at most one prize?
From 9 different coins, how many ways can 4 coins be selected?
How many 5-letter arrangements can be formed from the letters of BANANA?
questions.explanation: Select 5 letters and arrange with repetition of A and N: total distinct arrangements = 60.

questions.question: If 3 different books are to be arranged on a shelf and book A must be first, how many arrangements are possible?
questions.explanation: With A fixed first, the remaining 2 books can be arranged in 2! = 2 ways.

practiceProblems.question: In how many ways can 4 different trophies be arranged on a shelf?
A team of 3 is to be chosen from 10 players. How many possible teams are there?
